content.full_text:
May 19, 1975


A-75-179


Ms. Rose Elizabeth Bird, Secretary
Agricultura and Services Agency
1220 N Street
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Ms. Bird:

Re: Request for Opinion
No. 75-109

Thank you for your letter of April 30, 1975 requesting an
opinion with respect to the duties of the Agriculture and
Services Agency under the Political Reform Act.

No formal opinion will be issued in this matter because the
question of the level within your agency at which the conflict of
interest code must be formulated will soon be considered in a
more general context by the Commission. Government Code Section
87301 provides as follows:

It is the policy of this act that Conflict of
Interest Codes shall be formulated at the most
decentralized level possible, but without precluding
intra-departmental review. Any question of the level
of a department which should be deemed an "agency"
for the purposes of Section 87300 shall be resolved
by the code reviewing body.

In order to carry out its responsibilities under Section
87301 and to provide assistance to state and local agencies
regarding conflict of interest codes, the Commission will
establish a Conflict of Interest Division within its staff.
This division should be operational by August 1, 1975. Shortly
thereafter, the Commission should be prepared to deal with the
questions you have raised, and others related to conflict of
interest codes. We believe this will be timely, since the
deadline for development of conflict of interest codes will
not occur until April 1 of 1976.

Mrs. Rose Elizabeth Bird
May 19, 1975
Page Two


Both the Commission and the staff will welcome your ideas
and suggestions for implementing Section 87301 and the other
section relating to conflict of interest codes.

Sincerely,


Daniel H. Lowenstein
Chairman

DHL:cjb

STATE OF CALIFORNIA

Edmund G. Brown Jr.
GOVERNOR

AGRICULTURE AND SERVICES AGENCY

Request No. 75 109

1220 N STREET
SACRAMENTO 95814

Date Received 5-5-75

Response Due 5-19-75

April 30, 1975

Fair Political Practices Commission
924 J Street, Suite 522
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Commission Members:

This request for an opinion relates to the duties of the
Secretary of Agriculture and Services under Title 9
(commencing with Section 81000) of the Government Code.

The specific issue pertains to the provisions of Article 3
(commencing with Section 87300) of Chapter 7, relating to
the promulgation by state agencies of Conflict of Interest
Codes.

1. The Agriculture and Services Agency is responsible
for the administration of eleven departments and
other state agencies (Section 12804 of Chapter 2
commencing with Section 12850) of the Government
Code.

Question: Can a single approved Conflict of
Interest Code formulated in cooperation with the
eleven departments and other agencies be adopted
by the Agriculture and Services Agency to apply
to all employees within the Agency, including
those in the departments and other agencies,
thereby eliminating the necessity for each
separate department and other agency to adopt
its own code?

2. There are within the several departments of the
Agriculture and Services Agency boards established
by state law to administer certain state programs.
Specifically, within the Department of Food and
Agriculture advisory boards exist pursuant to the
provisions of the Marketing Act of 1937; see Article
8 (commencing with Section 58841) of the Food and

AGRICULTURE AND AGRICULTURE
CONSUMER AFFAIRS
DEPARTMENTS OF THE AGENCY
STATE PERSONNEL BOARD
GENERAL SERVICES
STRIAL RELATIONS
TEACHERS RETIREMENT SYSTEM
FIRE MARSHAL
FRANCHISE TAX BOARD
PUBLIC EMPLOYEES RETIREMENT SYSTEM
VETERANS AFFAIRS
COMMERCE

Fair Political Practices Commission
April 30, 1975
Page 2

Agricultural Code. Also, within the Department of
Consumer Affairs there are numerous boards concerned
with professional and occupational licensing, e.g.,
accounting (Sec. 5000, et seq.); barber examiners
(Sec. 6500, et seq.); dental examiners (Sec. 1601,
et seq.); landscape architects (Sec. 5620, et seq.)
of the Business and Professions Code.

Question: Assuming an approved Conflict of Interest
Code is adopted by the department, must each of the
boards adopt its own approved Conflict of Interest
Code?

Sincerely,

Rose Elizabeth Bird
Agency Secretary

sections.conclusion: No formal opinion will be issued in this matter because the question of the level within your agency at which the conflict of interest code must be formulated will soon be considered in a more general context by the Commission. Government Code Section 87301 provides that Conflict of Interest Codes shall be formulated at the most decentralized level possible, but without precluding intra-departmental review. The Commission will establish a Conflict of Interest Division to assist state and local agencies, and the deadline for development of conflict of interest codes will not occur until April of 1976.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether the Agriculture and Services Agency can adopt a single Conflict of Interest Code for all its departments and subordinate agencies, and whether boards within those departments must separately adopt their own codes.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declines to issue a formal opinion at this time because the specific question of the appropriate decentralized level for formulating conflict of interest codes will be addressed more comprehensively by the Commission. The FPPC references Government Code Section 87301 which mandates codes be formulated at the most decentralized level possible, and indicates a Conflict of Interest Division will be established to provide guidance.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: modern; LLM: Document contains both the incoming request (dated April 30, 1975) and the FPPC response (dated May 19, 1975). OCR quality is moderate with some character corruption. The FPPC response is a declination letter rather than a substantive opinion, explaining that the questions will be addressed in a broader context. No formal CONCLUSION section is present, so conclusion_synthetic is based on the substantive content of the response.

The Commission will establish a Conflict of Interest Division to assist state and local agencies, and the deadline for development of conflict of interest codes will not occur until April of 1976.

embedding.summary: The Agriculture and Services Agency requested opinions on whether a single Conflict of Interest Code could apply to all its departments and whether boards must separately adopt codes. The FPPC declined to issue a formal opinion, stating the matter would be addressed more generally in the future and directing the agency to Government Code Section 87301's requirement that codes be formulated at the most decentralized level possible.
